acceptance_targets: [CH15-B1] Reading snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:177-205, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:215-239, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:251-261, each exchange cluster must introduce a new pressure or loss of option. If any one cluster can be removed without losing a distinct beat, the revision fails.

[CH15-C1] Across the four cited follow-up beats, no more than two may explicitly name Mohit's tactic or summarize what he has just done. At least two must instead land through concrete reaction or room behavior that lets the coercive effect be inferred by reading the passage.

findings.acceptance_test: Reading snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:177-205, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:215-239, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:251-261, each exchange cluster must introduce a new pressure or loss of option. If any one cluster can be removed without losing a distinct beat, the revision fails.
findings.problem: Once the committee has effectively chosen deferral, the back half of the scene keeps restating provenance, timing, and contribution in near-synonymous loops. The result is procedural relitigation instead of escalating dramatic pressure, so the chapter spends tension after the outcome is already legible.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or merge one of the repeated objection loops centered on snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:177, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:193, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:215, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:239, then use the recovered space before snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:251 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:261 for a distinct irreversible beat: a concrete procedural consequence, a sharper faculty self-protection reveal, or one last tactical move from Leela that fails in a new way rather than another restatement of the same no.

findings.acceptance_test: Across the four cited follow-up beats, no more than two may explicitly name Mohit's tactic or summarize what he has just done. At least two must instead land through concrete reaction or room behavior that lets the coercive effect be inferred by reading the passage.
findings.problem: The narration repeatedly explains Mohit's rhetorical move immediately after he speaks. Those glosses are accurate, but the accumulation starts doing the reader's inferential work and weakens the scene's confidence in its own dramatization.
findings.rewrite_direction: In the explanatory follow-up paragraphs at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:145, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:157, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:205, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:239, keep only the sharpest one or two interpretive judgments. Replace the others with observable consequences in the room: Leela's involuntary body response, a committee member's note-taking or silence, or a procedural shift that lets the reader feel Mohit's tactic without being told how to parse it every time.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the absurdity and cruelty of graduate labor without converting the book into caricature or chase-plot menace. In edge cases, prefer the messier and more compromising version of events if it yields stronger human consequence and remains causally plausible; render sex, professional theft, bureaucratic harm, and emotional pettiness with the same specificity as any technical problem.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene turns vague at the exact point humiliation, erotic charge, class shame, visa fear, or institutional cruelty becomes concrete, rewrite until the cost is materially and socially legible.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High; contractions are the baseline in speech, and uncontracted forms should appear only for deliberate precision, class performance, emotional emphasis, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate public and private pressure, ensemble chapters and solitary ones, bureaucratic scenes and domestic scenes, deadline compression and aftermath inventory, so no two adjacent chapters rely on the same structural rhythm or dialogue density.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary literary English grounded in material detail; technical language should appear only when the social reality of the lab requires it, and it should never replace bodily or relational consequence.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person centered on Leela, with occasional brief widenings that register group mood and institutional atmosphere without leaving her interpretive frame.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate compressed observational lines with longer accrual sentences that can carry comedy, shame, and procedural dread in the same movement; quiet chapters should breathe, deadline chapters should tighten without turning telegraphic.
